programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

programs.name: Bronze Cross and Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving  Society. The Bronze Cross certification begins the transition from lifesaving  to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and  begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic  facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and  communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross  is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and  leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres  in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Standard First Aid (CPR C)  certification provides comprehensive first-aid training, covering spinal  injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest  injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the use of automated external  defibrillators. You must bring the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in  Bronze Medallion. This course is offered in an intensive weekend format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with  CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.  Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual  received in Bronze Medallion. For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds  for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

programs.name: Bronze Cross and Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving  Society. The Bronze Cross certification begins the transition from lifesaving  to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and  begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic  facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and  communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross  is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and  leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres  in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Standard First Aid (CPR C)  certification provides comprehensive first-aid training, covering spinal  injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest  injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the use of automated external  defibrillators. You must bring the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in  Bronze Medallion. This course is offered in an intensive weekend format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with  CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.  Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual  received in Bronze Medallion. For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds  for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2 - Small Group
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side gliding and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Young Artist
programs.description: Skill is paired with creativity in this introduction to fine arts. While exploring a variety of art materials, tools and styles, you will learn to draw, sketch and paint to let your inner artist shine.

programs.name: Bronze Cross
programs.description: Bronze Cross begins the transition from lifesaving to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. This course is generally offered in an extended nine-class format.Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in Bronze Medallion.

programs.name: Bronze Cross and Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving  Society. The Bronze Cross certification begins the transition from lifesaving  to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and  begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic  facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and  communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross  is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and  leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres  in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Standard First Aid (CPR C)  certification provides comprehensive first-aid training, covering spinal  injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest  injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the use of automated external  defibrillators. You must bring the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in  Bronze Medallion. This course is offered in an intensive weekend format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with  CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.  Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual  received in Bronze Medallion. For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds  for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Cooking: Baking (Women)
programs.description: Whether you are making breads, desserts or pastries; something savoury or sweet, this activity will help you reach new culinary delights. programs.description: Felting is a unique and long-standing art and craft. In this introductory activity you will learn how to bond fibers together using a combination of techniques such as use of pressure, rolling and with the aid of a needle to create personal works of art.

programs.name: Games: Billiards (Women)
programs.description: From active to interactive and simple to strategic, games are a great way to gain new skills. In billiards, a game played on a table where a cue is used to strike a ball to make a play, you will learn stance, aim, alignment and striking as well as game rules.

programs.name: Gardening
programs.description: Explore the outdoors. With hands-on activities, including projects and games, you will learn about the beauty of nature. This activity will include outdoor components.

programs.name: Knitting
programs.description: Participants in this program will learn the basics of knitting and the techniques involved.

programs.name: Making Music with Caregiver
programs.description: As you clap, sing, laugh and play and will discover making music is fun. Through a variety of activities, crafts and games you will learn the basics of rhythm and sounds. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Painting: Acrylic
programs.description: Create pictures with acrylic paint. Learn how to mix and choose colours, how to use tools to create different textures and add layers of paint to create depth.

programs.name: Painting: Mixed Media
programs.description: Create pictures with a variety of paint types. From oil and acrylic to tempera and watercolours, learn how and when to use different paints to create specific effects.

programs.name: Painting: Watercolour
programs.description: Create pictures with watercolour paint. Learn basic brush techniques as well as how to choose colours, build layers to show depth and shadow and apply washes for unique effects.

programs.name: Music: Singing - Private
programs.description: Would you benefit from private music lessons? This activity provides personal singing instruction with a one-on-one ratio. Here you will learn the importance of proper posture and warm-ups as well as voice projection.

programs.name: Preschool Swim 1: Penguin

programs.name: Preschool Swim 3: Otter
programs.description: Jump in and get your feet wet. Develop front and back swim. Learn side glides, sculling and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Guardian Swim 2.

programs.name: Science

programs.name: Soccer: House League
programs.description: Be part of a team! Our soccer house league activity is structured to teach you the basic skills and rules of the game, and then put them into action through recreational level competitive play. As you learn and develop soccer fundamentals, you will also learn the importance of teamwork and respectful play. Game times are built into scheduled classes, no additional days/times are required.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 2 - Small Group
programs.description: Jump into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front swim, back kicks and glides. Learn how to stay afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Work towards a continuous swim of five metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 1 or Preschool Swim  3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side glide and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 3 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop staying afloat in deep water, side gliding and kicking. Learn front crawl, diving and back swim. Work towards a continuous swim of 10 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 2 or Preschool Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 4
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front crawl and treading water. Learn side breathing, somersault, whip-kick and forward roll. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 metres. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 3.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Ultra Swim 5 - Small Group
programs.description: Dive into lessons to improve your swim skills. Develop front dive, forward roll and front crawl. Learn back crawl, eggbeater kick and elementary backstroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 75 metres. Class size is limited to four participants.Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Ultra Swim 4.

programs.name: Pilates

programs.name: Recreation Adventures
programs.description: Your recreational adventure begins here. Enjoy activities like sing-alongs, interactive games and arts and crafts while having fun with new friends.

programs.name: Sewing
programs.description: Come get an introduction to this handy skill. Learn how to read a pattern, cut material, stich by hand or use a sewing machine with the aim of completing a simple project.

programs.name: Stroke Improvement
programs.description: Work on stroke correction and improving technique through drills and progressions focused on front crawl, back crawl, elementary backstroke and breaststroke.

programs.name: Watercolour: All Levels
programs.description: Whether you’re just starting out or looking to  refine your skills, this course welcomes all levels! Learn the fundamentals of  watercolour painting, including washes, layering, blending and colour mixing.  Explore a variety of subjects while developing your own style and techniques.  With easy-to-follow demonstrations and plenty of hands-on practice, you’ll gain  confidence and creativity with every brushstroke.

programs.name: Wheel Work: Intermediate/Advanced
programs.description: Hone your wheel throwing talents through demonstration and individual instruction and discover how to shape forms from previously wheel thrown parts. Learn to critique clay forms and improve your technique independently. This is a course for potters with intermediate/advanced skills. It is not designed for beginner-level students. Students should be able to competently centre and throw a 2-lb cylinder.

programs.name: Wood Working
programs.description: Working with wood is a wonderful way to engage your mind and use your hands. From measuring and designing to cutting and shaping, there are many skills to gain. In this introductory activity, you will learn how to safely use tools and machinery to create your own woodworking project.

programs.name: Yoga with Caregiver

programs.name: Yoga: Chair
programs.description: Gain all the benefits of yoga with the added support of a chair or your walker. Combining gentle stretches and traditional poses, you will improve your flexibility, strength, breathing and relaxation with every movement.

programs.name: Dance: Ballet
programs.description: This classic style of dance is all about graceful movements. Learn basic ballet positions, postures and proper technique through various exercises and engaging choreography.

In pickleball, a fast-moving racquet sport that combines elements from tennis, badminton and table tennis, you will learn basics like serving, hitting and rallying as well as game rules through drills, practice and play.

programs.name: Adult Swim 1
programs.description: Build confidence in the water one stroke at a time. Learn front and back swim, front, side and back glides, going underwater, breathing effectively and staying afloat in deep water. Work towards a continuous swim of 25 to 50 metres.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Adult Swim 2
programs.description: Build confidence in deep water one stroke at a time. Develop front crawl, back crawl, glides and staying afloat in deep water. Learn stride entry, eggbeater kick and breaststroke. Work towards a continuous swim of 50 to 100 meters. Prerequisite: Successful completion of or skill ability equal to Adult Swim 1.

programs.name: Cardio Dance
programs.description: Cardio dance is a dynamic and exciting aerobic workout that anyone can enjoy. With a focus on various culturally inspired music and dance movements and a goal of lifting your heart rate you will dance your way to new fitness heights.

programs.name: Cooking: Baking (Women)
programs.description: Whether you are making breads, desserts or pastries; something savoury or sweet, this activity will help you reach new culinary delights. Come learn the basics of cooking and how to prepare delicious and nutritious recipes inspired by Canada’s Food Guide. From measuring and mixing ingredients to food safety, you will gain confidence in the kitchen.The City of Toronto and its staff cannot guarantee that the foods or supplies within its cooking programs are free of allergens. Notify staff at your location of any food allergies or dietary restrictions you may have.

programs.name: Dance: Line Dance
programs.description: Are you ready to step up your dance skills with line dancing? Using basic steps to create repetitive combinations, you'll learn moves like the shuffle, grapevine, coaster step and mambo step through various exercises and engaging choreography.

programs.name: Felting
programs.description: Felting is a unique and long-standing art and craft. In this introductory activity you will learn how to bond fibers together using a combination of techniques such as use of pressure, rolling and with the aid of a needle to create personal works of art.

programs.name: Games: Billiards (Women)
programs.description: From active to interactive and simple to strategic, games are a great way to gain new skills. In billiards, a game played on a table where a cue is used to strike a ball to make a play, you will learn stance, aim, alignment and striking as well as game rules.

programs.name: Gardening
programs.description: Explore the outdoors. With hands-on activities, including projects and games, you will learn about the beauty of nature. This activity will include outdoor components.

programs.name: Guardian Swim 1
programs.description: Swim together to promote confidence and safety in the water. Learn how to enter and exit the pool, go underwater, breathe effectively, and float with assistance. One caregiver per swimmer must participate in the water at all times.

programs.name: Knitting
programs.description: Participants in this program will learn the basics of knitting and the techniques involved.

programs.name: Making Music with Caregiver
programs.description: As you clap, sing, laugh and play and will discover making music is fun. Through a variety of activities, crafts and games you will learn the basics of rhythm and sounds. A caregiver must actively participate in this activity.

programs.name: Painting: Acrylic
programs.description: Create pictures with acrylic paint. Learn how to mix and choose colours, how to use tools to create different textures and add layers of paint to create depth.

programs.name: Painting: Mixed Media
programs.description: Create pictures with a variety of paint types. From oil and acrylic to tempera and watercolours, learn how and when to use different paints to create specific effects.

programs.name: Painting: Watercolour
programs.description: Create pictures with watercolour paint. Learn basic brush techniques as well as how to choose colours, build layers to show depth and shadow and apply washes for unique effects.

programs.name: Bronze Cross and Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving  Society. The Bronze Cross certification begins the transition from lifesaving  to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and  begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic  facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and  communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross  is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and  leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres  in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Standard First Aid (CPR C)  certification provides comprehensive first-aid training, covering spinal  injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest  injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the use of automated external  defibrillators. You must bring the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in  Bronze Medallion. This course is offered in an intensive weekend format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with  CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.  Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual  received in Bronze Medallion. For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds  for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion & Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and physically. Judgment, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on, and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Emergency First Aid (CPR-B) certification teaches victim assessment, CPR and addressing choking, as well as first aid treatment of external bleeding, heart attack, stroke and wound and burns. This course generally offered in an intensive weekend format.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Cross and Standard First Aid (CPR C)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving  Society. The Bronze Cross certification begins the transition from lifesaving  to lifeguarding. Candidates strengthen and expand their lifesaving skills and  begin to apply the principles and techniques of active surveillance in aquatic  facilities. Bronze Cross emphasizes the importance of teamwork and  communication in preventing and responding to aquatic emergencies. Bronze Cross  is a prerequisite for advanced training in the Society’s National Lifeguard and  leadership certification programs. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres  in 11 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Standard First Aid (CPR C)  certification provides comprehensive first-aid training, covering spinal  injuries, heat/cold injuries, bone and joint injuries, abdominal and chest  injuries, burns, medical emergencies, and the use of automated external  defibrillators. You must bring the Canadian Lifesaving manual received in  Bronze Medallion. This course is offered in an intensive weekend format. Prerequisite: Bronze Medallion and Emergency First Aid with  CPR-B (current or expired) or Standard First Aid with CPR-C  from an approved provider.  Equipment: Bring your copy of the Canadian Lifesaving manual  received in Bronze Medallion. For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds  for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

programs.name: Bronze Medallion & Emergency First Aid (CPR B)
programs.description: This course is offered in collaboration with the Lifesaving Society. Bronze Medallion challenges the candidate both mentally and physically. Judgment, knowledge, skill and fitness – the four components of water rescue – form the basis of Bronze Medallion training. Candidates acquire the assessment and problem-solving skills needed to make good decisions in, on, and around the water. Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Emergency First Aid (CPR-B) certification teaches victim assessment, CPR and addressing choking, as well as first aid treatment of external bleeding, heart attack, stroke and wound and burns. This course generally offered in an intensive weekend format.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.

Bronze Medallion is a prerequisite for assistant lifeguard training in Bronze Cross. Candidates must be able to swim 400 metres in 12 minutes or less by the course exam date. The Emergency First Aid (CPR-B) certification teaches victim assessment, CPR and addressing choking, as well as first aid treatment of external bleeding, heart attack, stroke and wound and burns. This course generally offered in an intensive weekend format.Prerequisite: Minimum age of 13 years old or Bronze Star. Candidates with Bronze Star, that are under 13 years of age, can register for this course by calling 416-396-7378 or visiting an in-person registration site.For information about attendance, withdrawals and refunds for courses offered by the Lifesaving Society, visit www.toronto.ca/recskills.
